channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: In Jamaica, Marco Rubio vows to revisit US travel warning
item.description: KINGSTON, Jamaica (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Wednesday the State Department will re-evaluate its travel warnings for Jamaica and other countries to ensure they reflect real conditions on the ground.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Crowds flock to newest Catholic saint in Assisi
item.description: ASSISI, Italy (AP) — Pilgrims have been pouring into this medieval hilltop town to venerate not only two of the Catholic Church’s most celebrated saints, Francis and Clare, but its newest — Carlo Acutis, the first millennial saint, who will be canonized on April 27. “St. Francis, St.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked people handcuffing her
item.description: SOMERVILLE, Mass. (AP) — A Turkish national and doctoral student at Tufts University has been detained by U.S. Department of Homeland Security agents without explanation, her lawyer said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, KATHY McCORMACK and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Pilot rescued with 2 girls from icy Alaska lake was not authorized to have passengers, official says
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — A pilot rescued with two young family members after they survived a night on the wing of an airplane partially submerged in an Alaska lake is a student pilot who was not authorized to fly with passengers, according to a U.S. official and federal aviation records.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ICE arrestees claim they were targeted for protest involvement
item.description: Students who acknowledge they participated in pro-Palestinian protests are being targeted by ICE, a complaint says
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Kansas measles cases double to 23 and new Ohio outbreak sickens 10
item.description: A measles outbreak in Kansas doubled in less than a week to 23 cases and has "a possible link” to outbreaks in Texas and New Mexico that have sickened more than 370, the state health department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday on Capitol Hill over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kids 'coordinated a plan' to kill mom after she turned off the Wi-Fi
item.description: "The three siblings allegedly coordinated a plan to try and kill the mother," spokesman Ed Gonzalez wrote in a Facebook post.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Teens arrested after tractor-trailers struck by objects dropped from overpass
item.description: Two juvenile males, aged 16 and 17, have been charged with reckless endangerment after they reportedly threw multiple objects from an I-640 overpass.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, NewsNation

item.title: Human remains found inside of car pulled from Fox River in Illinois
item.description: Karen Schepers and her Toyota Celica went missing around 1 a.m. on April 16, 1983, after leaving a bar following a night out with co-workers.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, NewsNation

item.title: Kermit the Frog to give commencement address at University of Maryland
item.description: Prominent political figures and lawmakers on the commencement speaker circuit are about to have some friendly, froggy competition.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICE in Boston nabs previously deported Dominican fentanyl trafficker, child rape convict, murderers
item.description: ICE agents in the Boston area have arrested previously deported fentanyl migrant traffickers, a child rape convict and several migrants wanted for murder.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How the Signal 'group chat' furor has affected the 5 biggest players
item.description: The fact that such inherently sensitive information was being communicated in such a forum — and in front of a reporter whom no other participant appears to have noticed — has caused a political storm.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KRON4

item.title: Former US attorney Jessica Aber suspected to have died from 'natural causes': police
item.description: The former U.S. attorney who was found dead in Alexandria, Virginia, over the weekend is suspected to have died from "natural causes," according to police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wildfires in North and South Carolina fueled by drought, wind and fallen trees from Hurricane Helene
item.description: Dry conditions, wind and trees downed by Hurricane Helene fueled wildfires in North Carolina and South Carolina, where evacuation orders were in effect Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bureau of Indian Affairs Missing and Murdered Unit investigating alleged military base killing
item.description: The Bureau of Indian Affairs Missing and Murdered Unit aids in the arrest, and indictment of an active-duty airman accused of murdering a 21-year-old tribal woman at an Air Force base.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video and 911 calls played by police detail chaos from deadly shooting in New Mexico
item.description: Police released video footage and 911 emergency calls Tuesday depicting chilling scenes of people running in panic as gunfire crackled in a New Mexico park where three people were killed and 15 others were wounded during an unauthorized car show.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT and SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gunman who killed 23 in racist attack at Texas Walmart is offered plea deal to avoid death penalty
item.description: The gunman who killed 23 people in a racist attack targeting Hispanic shoppers at a Walmart near the U.S.-Mexico border in 2019 would avoid the death penalty under a plea offer announced Tuesday, abruptly ending years of efforts by prosecutors to see that he face execution by lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A new Trump portrait for Colorado's Capitol could take time after one he disliked was removed
item.description: Filling an empty space on the wall of presidential portraits in the Colorado Capitol with a new painting of Donald Trump could take time after one he disliked was removed and put into storage.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Columbia student protester can't be detained for now as she fights deportation, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Columbia University student from South Korea who is facing potential deportation for her involvement in a pro-Palestinian protest can't be taken into immigration detention for now, a federal judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House Democrats demand answers from Trump team about war plans Signal chat
item.description: The top Democrats on the House’s Defense, Oversight, Foreign Affairs, and Intelligence committees want answers from President Trump’s national security team.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: The MLB teams with the highest
item.description: A California team has the highest median ticket price, a new report found, but they don't play in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Trump signs order targeting law firm that employed Mueller team prosecutor
item.description: President Trump signed an order Tuesday targeting a law firm that previously employed Andrew Weissmann, a former federal prosecutor who worked on then-special counsel Robert Mueller’s team.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: FBI launches Tesla vandalism task force
item.description: The task force seeks to counter the violent activity targeting Tesla vehicles and charging stations in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Student returns lost backpack filled with luxury jewelry, diamonds, cash
item.description: Amid the fog of international travel, a group of Jewish students studying in Jerusalem was handed a backpack -- that none recognized as their own.
item.dc:creator: Anthony DiLorenzo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: American couple imprisoned in Mexico, daughter says
item.description: Paul and Christy Akeo, a couple from Spring Arbor, Michigan, are currently being held in a maximum-security Mexican prison, their family said.
item.dc:creator: Taryn Simmons, KTLA 5

item.title: USC star JuJu Watkins suffers season-ending knee injury in March Madness win over Mississippi State
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — JuJu Watkins was carried off the floor with a season-ending knee injury in the first quarter of top-seeded Southern California’s game against Mississippi State in the NCAA Tournament on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Consider deleting your 23andMe data now, California AG urges
item.description: The genetic testing company has the genetic data of more than15 million customers — and California Attorney General Rob Bonta has advised users to purge their data.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Columbia student sues Trump admin over efforts to deport her
item.description: Columbia University student Yunseo Chung is suing the Trump administration amid its efforts to deport the 21-year-old, who has been in the U.S. since she was 7 and has lawful permanent resident status.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump officials' war planning group chat breach
item.description: Washington was rocked Monday by a truly extraordinary story. The editor in chief of The Atlantic, Jeffrey Goldberg, recounted how he had — apparently inadvertently — been added to a group chat featuring some of the most senior members of the United States government.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he knows nothing of Atlantic journalist swept up in war plan text chain
item.description: President Trump said Monday he knew nothing of a report by a journalist for The Atlantic who says he was swept up in a text message chain with defense and national security officials while they detailed plans for an attack on Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Judge won't lift block on Trump use of Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge James Boasberg declined to lift a restraining order barring the Trump administration from using the Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans, determining migrants are likely to succeed in pushing for hearings on whether they are in a gang.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Maps: The states, counties with the most excessive drinkers, per new report
item.description: "Excessive drinking" includes binge drinking, heavy drinking, drinking while pregnant, and underage drinking, according to the CDC.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Georgia jury orders Monsanto parent to pay nearly $2.1 billion in Roundup weedkiller lawsuit
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A jury in Georgia has ordered Monsanto parent Bayer to pay nearly $2.1 billion in damages to a man who says the company's Roundup weed killer caused his cancer, according to attorneys representing the plaintiff.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Latest: Mass layoffs in the federal government raises concerns of potential espionage
item.description: As President Donald Trump and billionaire Elon Musk work to overhaul the federal government, they’re forcing out thousands of workers with insider knowledge and connections who now need a job.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Takeaways from AP's report on sexual misconduct in US statehouses
item.description: The #MeToo movement hit critical mass in 2017, toppling politicians and public figures across the country. Allegations of harassment and misconduct — some from many years prior — emerged. Many state legislatures responded by adopting or enhancing policies against sexual misconduct.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 killed and 15 hurt in a shooting at a park in New Mexico’s Las Cruces
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — Three people were fatally shot, and 15 others were hurt, after an altercation broke out at a park in the desert city of Las Cruces, police said Saturday. Police and fire crews arrived at just after 10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH and MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, WGN 9
